title: Missed Optimization: umul.with.overflow(x, C0).overflow || (prod > C1) —> x > C1 // C0
summary:
The original issue is a very specific redundant-overflow pattern:

- `%mwo = umul.with.overflow(x, C1)`
- extract `%mul` and `%ovf`
- compute `icmp ugt %mul, C2`
- return `or %ovf, %cmp`

Semantically, this asks: “is the full mathematical product `x*C1` greater than `C2`?” If overflow happens, the answer is already true for sufficiently large products, so the pair `(overflow || truncated_product > C2)` can be reduced to a single compare on the original operand. In the motivating case, that becomes `icmp ugt x, floor(C2 / C1)`.

The patch generalizes this into `foldOrUnsignedUMulOverflowICmp()` in InstCombine. It pattern-matches a `umul_with_overflow` with one constant operand, matches the two `extractvalue`s, requires the compare to be `icmp ugt` on the result value against a constant, accepts either operand order in the `or` via `m_c_Or`, and only folds when the compare is one-use and `C1 != 0`. The rewrite is:
`or ovf, icmp ugt mul, C2  -->  icmp ugt x, udiv(C2, C1)`.

The dev tests broaden coverage well beyond the original scalar i64 case: constant-zero multiplier, commuted `or`, `or disjoint`, different bitwidth (`i8`), vector splats, non-splat vectors, poison lanes, and several negative tests where folding must not happen (`ult`, wrong boolean op `and`, multi-use overflow/cmp/mul/call result). This is a good template for turning a bug-triggering peephole into a reusable InstCombine fold plus robustness tests.
